title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown will miss the Celtics' game against the Atlanta Hawks due to left Achilles tendinitis.
Head coach Joe Mazzulla described Brown's condition as "day-to-day" after a 31-point game against the Thunder.
Brown has been crucial for the Celtics this season, averaging 28.6 points, a career high, and helping achieve a 48-24 record.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington are phasing out gas-powered leaf blowers due to noise and emissions concerns.
A Boston.com poll shows 73% of readers believe gas-powered leaf blowers should remain allowed.
Opponents criticize the bans for being impractical, while supporters cite noise and health concerns.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The BA.3.2 variant, first detected in South Africa in 2024, has been identified in Massachusetts wastewater.
BA.3.2 is evading current vaccines targeting JN.1 strains, raising concerns of potential U.S. summer COVID-19 surge.
The World Health Organization finds BA.3.2 poses low additional public health risk and hasn't increased hospitalizations.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ruled that two Massachusetts DCF employees could stand trial for the alleged unreasonable seizure of two children.
The lawsuit claims the DCF workers removed the children without a warrant, despite no evidence of abuse being found.
DCF became involved after a hospital visit revealed a rib fracture, but an investigation found no abuse evidence.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Willie's on Beacon Hill is now open, offering pizzas and global appetizers from chef Jamie Bissonnette.
Ladyfish in Portland opens May 1, featuring seafood dishes influenced by Chinese and Italian cuisines.
La Tavernetta, a coastal tavern by chef Douglass Williams, is set to open this spring in East Boston.

title: The Boston Public Library is the star of Kate Quinn’s latest NYT bestseller
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Kate Quinn's novel 'The Astral Library' features the Boston Public Library as a magical portal.
'The Astral Library' blends fantasy with Quinn's love for Boston and debuted at No. 2 on the NYT bestseller list.
Quinn's book emphasizes libraries as community spaces; next work returns to historical fiction, focusing on UK suffragettes.

title: Councilors, Pressley doubling down on demands for bodycam footage after deadly police shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston City Council approved a resolution urging police to allow oversight officials timely access to bodycam footage after shootings.
The resolution follows a controversial manslaughter charge against Officer Nicholas O’Malley after a fatal shooting in Roxbury.
U.S. Rep. Ayanna Pressley and councilors demand footage release, emphasizing transparency and accountability for the involved families.

title: New England’s only dual-launch straddle coaster to open at Six Flags
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Quantum Accelerator, New England's only dual-launch straddle coaster, opens April 11 at Six Flags New England.
The coaster features two launches, reaching speeds of 30 and 45 mph, and includes 11 airtime moments.
Quantum Accelerator is Six Flags New England's twelfth roller coaster and the newest since The Joker in 2017.

title: A new program will make Boston the 1st major-city school district to require AI training
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston will require AI training for all public high school graduates beginning September 2026, becoming the first major U.S. city to do so.
The AI program is supported by a $1 million grant from Paul English, aiming to train teachers at Boston's high schools.
The curriculum, developed by UMass Boston, emphasizes ethical and critical engagement with AI and includes course opportunities at the university.

title: After mix-up, Matthew Farwell’s lawyers amend court filing, say unnamed person fathered Sandra Birchmore’s unborn child
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matthew Farwell's lawyers amended a filing, clarifying an unnamed person, not his brother, fathered Sandra Birchmore's unborn child.
Prosecutors allege Farwell killed Birchmore and staged her suicide after she claimed he was the father of her child.
Matthew Farwell, held since 2024, has pleaded not guilty to charges of killing Birchmore; his lawyers seek his release on bail.
